No pools detected on this server.
Hardening is optional. ZFS NAS is fully functional without it — the service account already uses passwordless sudo for the commands it needs. This tab lets you restrict that sudo access to a reviewed allow-list of exact commands instead of broad access, and review any drift.

Apply it only for high-security deployments where you want to minimise the portal's host privileges. For most home / single-admin setups it can be safely skipped.

These packages are not required for core ZFS NAS functionality but enable additional features.

iSCSI block storage sharing
Packages: targetcli-fb
Not installed ⋮
S3-compatible object storage server
Packages: minio, mc
Not installed ⋮
UPS battery monitoring & protection
Packages: nut, nut-client
Not installed ⋮
Disk Power Management
Package: hdparm
Not installed ⋮
VM/LXC remote ZFS Backups (syncoid)
Package: sanoid (provides /usr/sbin/syncoid)
Not installed
VMs & Containers Support
Packages: lxd, lxd-client, lxd-agent, qemu-system-x86, qemu-kvm, bridge-utils, dnsmasq-base
Not installed BETA ⋮

The following packages are required for ZFS NAS to operate. All commands run with sudo.

Checking…
✓ All prerequisites installed
⚠
ZFS kernel module not loaded
zfsutils-linux is installed but the ZFS kernel module is not active. ZFS pools and datasets will not be accessible until the module is loaded.
A reboot is recommended to let the kernel load the ZFS module automatically. Alternatively, run sudo modprobe zfs manually.

                Features
                
              
              

                
                
                
                
              

            


            
            

              Startup / Shutdown
              

                

                  
                  
                

                

                  
                  
                

              

            



          

          

            
            
          

        

      


      
      

        

          

            
Deploy a Compose Stack

            
          

          

          


            
            

              
Where should this stack run?

              

                

                  
📦

                  
Fresh LXC container

                    
A new isolated container running Podman (the current default).

                

                

                  
🧩

                  
Existing Docker / Podman instance

                    
Add this stack to an LXC or VM that already runs Docker or Podman.

                

                

                  
🖥️

                  
Fresh virtual machine

                    
A new VM provisioned with Docker or Podman, then your stack deployed.

                

              

            


            
            

              

                

                Detecting Docker / Podman targets…
              

              

              

                No running instance with Docker or Podman was found.
Create a fresh LXC or VM instead.
              

            


            
            

              

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

              

                Environment file (.env) optional
                
              

            


          

          

            
            
            
            
          

        

      


      
      

        

          

            
Container Features

            
          

          


            

              
Unprivileged Container Recommended

              

                The container's root user (UID 0) is mapped to a non-root UID on the host (e.g. UID 100000). Even if a process escapes the container, it has no host privileges.
              

              

                ✓ Pros:
                 Stronger isolation, safer for multi-tenant hosts, immune to most container-escape exploits.
              

              

                ✗ Cons:
                 Some legacy software that requires real root (e.g. raw socket operations) may not work. UID mapping adds a small overhead.
              

            


            

              
Privileged Container Use with caution

              

                The container's root runs as real UID 0 on the host. No UID mapping is applied. This is the opposite of an unprivileged container.
              

              

                ✓ Pros:
                 Maximum compatibility — software that needs real kernel-level access works without modification.
              

              

                ✗ Cons:
                 A container escape gives an attacker full root on the host. Only use on trusted workloads on isolated hosts.
              

            


            

              
Nesting

              

                Allows running another container engine (Docker, Podman, LXD, etc.) inside this container. Enables nested virtualisation and CI/CD workflows.
              

              

                ✓ Pros:
                 Run Docker or Kubernetes inside a container without a full VM. Ideal for self-hosted CI runners.
              

              

                ✗ Cons:
                 Increases the attack surface. Nested containers share the host kernel. Combine with AppArmor or seccomp for defence-in-depth.
              

            


            

              
keyctl

              

                Enables the keyctl() system call inside the container, granting access to the Linux kernel keyring service.
              

              

                ✓ Use when:
                 Running SSSD, Kerberos, PAM modules, or any application that uses the kernel keyring for credential and secret caching.
              

              

                ✗ Note:
                 Blocked by default in unprivileged containers. Enabling it allows the container's root to create keyrings, which may be a risk on multi-tenant hosts.
              

            


            

              
FUSE

              

                Exposes /dev/fuse inside the container, allowing the use of FUSE (Filesystem in Userspace) mounts.
              

              

                ✓ Use when:
                 Running SSHFS, rclone (mount mode), gvfs, Flatpak, AppImage, or any tool that mounts a FUSE-based filesystem.
              

              

                ✗ Note:
                 Without this, all FUSE mount attempts fail with "Operation not permitted".
              

              

                ⚠ Lock issue warning: Enabling FUSE can cause filesystem lock problems in some container workloads — particularly when FUSE mounts are left mounted across container restarts or when multiple processes compete for the same mount point. Only enable this feature if your workload explicitly requires FUSE mounts.
              

            


            

              
Auto-start on Boot

              

                The container starts automatically when the host boots. The startup order and delay (in Startup / Shutdown) control sequencing between containers.
              

              

                ✓ Pros:
                 Zero-touch recovery after a reboot or power failure.
              

              

                ✗ Cons:
                 All auto-start containers compete for resources at once on reboot. Use startup order / delay to stagger heavy workloads.
              

            


          

          

            
          

        

      


      
      

        

          

            
💿 Manage ISOs

            
          

          

            

              
              
              
              
              

                
                

                  
                  
                

                
              

            

            

              
Uploading…

              

                

              

            

            

            

              
Select a pool to view ISOs.

            

          

          

            
          

        

      


      
      

        

          

            
🌐 Fetch ISO from URL

            
          

          

            

            

              Paste a direct link to an ISO image. ZNAS downloads it on the server,
              checks it ends in .iso with an ISO 9660 magic, and drops
              the file into the selected pool's ISO library.
            

            

              
              
—

            

            

              
              
            

            

              
              
            

            

              Tip: large downloads run in the background. Close this popup any
              time — progress shows up under Activity & Events.
            

          

          

            
            
          

        

      


      
      

        

          

            
Creating…

            
          

          

            

            

            
Closing this window leaves the task running in the background — track it in the Activity panel (bell icon).

Loading…

            

          

        

      


      
      

        

          

            
Import from Proxmox

            
          


          
          

            
Connect to a remote Proxmox host and list its VMs. Running and stopped VMs can both be selected for import.

            

            

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

            

            

              ℹ Credentials are used only for this import and are never stored on disk.
            

            

              
              
            

          


          
          

            

              

                
                
              

              

              

                

                  
                  
                

                

                  
                  
                

                

                  
                

              

            

            

              

                
                  

                    
                    VMID
                    VM Name
                    Status
                    vCPU
                    RAM
                    Disks
                    NICs
                  

                
                
              

            

            

              ℹ MAC addresses are preserved. Stop running VMs before importing for a consistent disk snapshot. Passthrough devices (USB/PCI) are skipped.
            

            

              
              
            

          


          
          

            

            

              
            

            

              Import running in background…

Create Network Bridge

            
          

          

          

            

              
              
              
Lowercase letters, digits, hyphens; max 15 chars

            

            

              
              
            

            

              
              
            

            
            

              

                

                  
                  
                

                

                  
                  
                

              

              

                
                
              

            

            
            

              

                
                
              

              

                

                  
                  
                

                

                  
                  
                  
Max 15 characters — must differ from the bridge name above

                

              

            

            
            

              
              
              
Default 1500. Use 9000 for jumbo frames.

Edit Bridge — 

            
          

          

          

            

              
              
            

            

            

              
Common Settings

              

            

            

              Advanced / Other Keys
              

              
            

            

          

          

            
            
          

        

      


      
      

        

          

            
Delete Bridge

            
          

          

            

              
⚠ This action cannot be undone

              
Bridge  will be permanently removed. If a VLAN interface was created by ZNAS, it will also be removed from /etc/network/interfaces.

            

          

          

            
            
          

        

      


      
      

        

          

            
Delete VLAN Interface

            
          

          

            

              
⚠ This action cannot be undone

              
VLAN interface  will be removed from the kernel and its stanza deleted from /etc/network/interfaces.

            

          

          

            
            
          

        

      


      
      

        

          

            
Edit MTU — 

            
          

          

          

            

              
              
              
Standard: 1500 · Jumbo frames: 9000

            

          

          

            
            
          

        

      


      
      

        

          

            
User Settings

            
          

          

            

              
              
              
Page shown automatically after login

            

            

              
              
              
How the Compute sidebar groups instances. By Tag lists each instance under every tag it carries.

            

            
Auto Theme

            
When the theme is set to Auto, ZNAS uses the day theme during daylight hours (OS light mode) and the night theme otherwise.

Loading…

            
          

        

      


      
      

        

          

            
Add Datastore

            
          

          

          

            

              
              
              
Only ZFS pools not already configured as a datastore are shown. One datastore per ZFS pool.

            

            

              
              

                pool/
                
              

              
Dataset created under the ZFS pool to hold this datastore's instances and volumes. Default incus-<hostname>.

            

            

              
              
              
Identifier used by Incus for this storage pool

            

          

          

            
            
          

        

      


      
      

        

          

            
Delete Datastore

            
          

          

          

            

              
⚠ This action cannot be undone

              
Datastore  will be removed from LXD. The underlying ZFS pool will not be deleted.

            

          

          

            
            
          

        

      


      
      

        

          

            

              
Edit Datastore

              

            

            
          

          

            

            

              
              
            

            

              
Volume Defaults (ZFS)

              

                
                
                
Applied to each new volume created in this datastore.

Create Snapshot

            
          

          

            

            

              
              
            

            

              
              

                Saving live memory may take 1–3 minutes depending on RAM. The VM will be paused during the save.

              ⚙️ Backup Schedule
              
              — 
            

            
          

          

            

            
⚠ Selected destination is the same pool as the source — this backup will give no protection against pool loss. Pick a different datastore for real protection.

            
⚠ syncoid is not installed on this host. Install it from the Prerequisites page (ZFS Replication row) to enable backups.

            

            

              
              
              

            


            

              
              

                Every
                
                
              

            


            

              
              
            


            

              
              
            


            

              
              

                
                :
                
              

            


            

              
              

                
                
              

            


            

              
              
            


            


            

              
              
            

          

        

      


      
      

        

          

            

              🛠 Restore Backup
              
            

            
          

          

            


            
            


            
            

              
              
            


            
            

              

                
                
              

              
Lands on datastore — (fixed — a ZFS clone can't cross pools)

            


            
            

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

            


            
            


            

              
              
            

          

        

      


      
      

        

          

            
🗎 Promote to Full Copy 

            
          

          

            

            

              Makes a full independent copy of this instance’s current state into the datastore you choose, then frees the backup it depends on. Any changes made since the instant restore are kept.
            

            

              ⚠ The instance must be Stopped before it can be promoted. Stop it first.
            

            

              
              
            

            

              
              
            

          

        

      


      
      

        

          

            

              Snapshot
              
              
            

            
          


          
          

            

            

              ZFS note: This is not the latest snapshot — reverting requires removing subsequent snapshots.
              
            

            

              
              

                

                  
                

                

                  
Revert to this snapshot

                  
Roll back the instance to this point in time

                

              

              
              

                

                  
                

                

                  
Clone as new instance

                  
Create a separate copy from this snapshot

                

              

              
              

                

                  
                

                

                  
Delete this snapshot

                  
Permanently remove — cannot be undone

                

              

            

          


          
          

            

              
              

                
Clone as new instance

                
from snapshot

Move Disk

              

            

            
          

          

            

            

              
              
              

                The instance must be stopped while the disk moves. Progress streams to the Activity & Events bar; cancel from there if needed.
                ⚠ This is the root disk — moving it migrates the entire instance to the new datastore.
              

            

            

              
              
            

          

        

      


      
      

        

          

            

              
Moving Disk…

              

            

            
          

          

            

            

              
              
            

          

        

      


      
      

        

          

            

              
Move Storage

              

            

            
          

          

            

            

              
              
              
The instance must be stopped. All volumes will be migrated to the selected datastore.

Configure the timezone used for scheduling and log timestamps.

          

          

          

            
            
          


          
        


        
        

          
Folder TreeMap Schedule

          
Automatically refresh the Folders TreeMap for every pool. Pools are scanned one after the other and each scan is logged in the activity log.

Real-time toast notifications pushed to all browser sessions currently open in this portal. No external server required.

Push notifications via ntfy.sh or a self-hosted ntfy server.

Send RFC 3164 syslog messages to a remote syslog server over UDP or TCP.

          

          

          

            
            

              

              

                

                

                  
                  
                

                

                  
                  
                

              

              

            

          

          

            
            

          

          

            
            

          

          

            
            
          

        


        


        
        


        
        

          

            

              
Encryption Key Management

Loading…

          

        


        
        

          

            
Certificate Management

            
          

          

            ⚠ Certificate change pending restart — a restart is required to apply the new certificate.
            
          

          

            
Loading…

          

        


        
        

          

            

              
Web Login Timeout

              
Browser sessions are saved encrypted on disk and survive a server restart in either mode.

            

            
          

          

            
          

          

            
            

              
              
            

            5 minutes minimum, 7 days maximum
          

          

          

            
          

        


        


        
        


          
          

            
VMs & Container Metrics

            
            

              Endpoint status: loading…
            

            

              
              
            

          


          
          

            
Listener / API

            

              
              
            

            

              
              
            

            

              
              
            

          


          
          

            
Image management

            

              
              
            

            
            

              
              
            

            

              
              
            

            

              
              
            

          


          
          

            
Backup compression

            

              
              
            

            

              
              
            

          



          
          

            
Docker Detection

            

              When enabled, ZNAS probes each VM or LXC you open for a running Docker daemon and renders the containers/stacks it finds on the instance's main screen. Stacks are grouped by their com.docker.compose.project.config_files label so you can Stop / Start / Restart / Update / Edit each project as a unit.
              


              Works only on instances where the LXD Terminal opens without a password — for LXC, that is any running container; for VMs, the incus-agent must be running inside the guest (Linux cloud images include it; Windows VMs and stripped-down Linuxes do not). Instances without the agent are silently skipped.
            

            
            
            

              
              
            

          


          
          

            

              
Memory Compression
                
              

              unknown
            


            
            

              Loading…
            


            
            


            
            

              
zram-tools is not installed

              
This card needs the Debian zram-tools package (~30 KB) to manage the compressed swap device. The package only adds a systemd unit and a config file; it doesn't change kernel parameters by itself.

              
              

            


            
            

              

              

                
Profile

                

                  
                  
                  
                  
                

              


              

                
                
                
              

            


            
            

              
Live stats

              

            


            
            

              ⓘ Why this matters / how to choose
              

                
What it does. Allocates a percentage of physical RAM as a compressed swap device (/dev/zram0). The kernel uses it before any disk swap, so cold pages from VMs end up compressed in RAM instead of hitting NVMe.

                
When to enable. If your VMs rarely peak past their allocations, leave it off. If you've seen the host swap to disk or VMs OOM-killed, turn it on.

                
Picking the percentage. Allocate more % when you want to over-commit RAM (run more VMs than physical RAM allows); allocate less when you only want a small safety net. Idle systems pay almost nothing — the % is the maximum pool size, not the running cost.

                
Algorithm. zstd is the default — best ratio (~3.5:1 typical), tiny CPU cost. lz4 is the fastest with a worse ratio (~2:1), pick it only if CPU is constrained. lzo is legacy.

                
Costs. CPU: zstd uses ~2–4 % of one core under steady swap pressure, basically zero when idle. Latency: ~10 µs per page from zram vs ~100 µs–1 ms from disk swap, so this is always faster than disk. RAM: only used when the kernel actually swaps.

                
What it doesn't fix. It can't replace adding RAM if your workload genuinely needs more — watch the swap-in rate. It can't help with memory leaks inside guests. It doesn't affect ZFS ARC (which lives in unswappable kernel memory).

              

            

          


          
          

            
Outbound proxy
              
            

            

              
              
            

            

              
              
            

            

              
              
            

            

              
              
            

          

        


      


    


    
    

      

      

        ⌨
        
        

        
        ⚙
        ▼

        Terminal
        

        
        
      

      

    


    
    


    
    

      

        ⚠️
        

          
Incus daemon not responding

          
ZNAS' incus commands are timing out — see troubleshooting docs.

        

        
      

    


    
    

      

      

        📋
        Activity & Events
        
        

        
        

          
          
        

        ▲
      

      

        
        

          

          

            
Loading…

          

        

        
        

          

            
No active alerts.

          

        

      

    


  









  

    

      

        
Add Capacity to Pool

        
      

      
    

    

      

      

        Select the disk(s) to add. All data on selected disks will be erased.
      

      

        
Loading…

      


      
      

        
How to add these disks:

        

      

    

    

      
      
    

  








  

    

      
Add Spare Disk

      
    

    

      

      

        A hot spare sits idle until a pool disk fails. ZFS will then automatically use it to resilver a replacement, keeping the pool protected without manual intervention.
      

      

        
        
      

    

    

      
      
    

  







  

    

      
Remove Spare Disk

      
    

    

      

      
Remove spare  from pool ?

      

        The disk will be unassigned from the pool and become available again. This will fail if the spare is currently being used to replace a failed disk.
      

    

    

      
      
    

  







  

    

      
Attach Mirror Disk

      
    

    

      

      
Attach a new mirror partner to  in pool .

      

        ZFS will immediately start mirroring (resilver) the selected disk. The pool stays online during the process. Use this to restore or create a mirror after a disk was detached.
      

      

        
        
      

    

    

      
      
    

  







  

    

      
Detach Disk

      
    

    

      

      
Detach  from pool ?

      

        This permanently removes the disk from the pool. Only possible when the disk is a mirror member and the pool will remain intact after removal.
      

    

    

      
      
    

  







  

    

      
Replace Disk

      
    

    

      

      
Replace  in pool  with:

      

        
Loading…

      

      

        ZFS will immediately start a resilver on the new disk. The pool remains accessible during the process.
      

    

    

      
      
    

  






  

    

      
⚠ Force Disk Offline

      
    

    

      

      
You are about to take disk  offline in pool .

      

        When to use this:

        • Replacing a failing or degraded disk without shutting down the server.

        • Temporarily removing a disk for maintenance while the pool remains online.

        • The pool must have enough redundancy (mirror, RAIDZ) to tolerate the missing disk.
      

      

        ⚠ A striped pool (no redundancy) will become DEGRADED and data may be inaccessible. Only proceed if you understand the consequences.
      

    

    

      
      
    

  







  

    

      
✦ Pool Fixer Wizard

      
    

    

      

      

    

    

      
      
    

  







  

    

      
⚠ Confirm Pool Creation

      
    

    

      

        Creating pool  will
        permanently erase all data on the following disk(s):
      

      

      

        This action cannot be undone. Make sure you have selected the correct disks and that they contain no data you wish to keep.
      

    

    

      
      
    

  







  

    

      

        
        Disconnect ZFS Pool
      

      
    

    

      

      

        

          
        

        

          
What this does

          

            Exporting a pool gracefully disconnects it from this system. All active I/O is flushed, datasets are unmounted, and the pool is cleanly taken offline.
          

          

            The pool and all its data remain intact on disk — it can be re-imported later on this system or moved to another machine.
          

        

      

      

        
⚠ Impact

        
    
          
  • All datasets on this pool will be unmounted immediately
    • 
          
  • Active SMB / NFS shares on this pool will become unavailable
    • 
          
  • Running applications reading from this pool will lose access
    • 
          
  • The pool will disappear from this portal until re-imported
    • 
        

      

      

        
        
      

    

    

      
      
    

  






  

    

      
⚠ Destroy ZFS Pool

      
    

    

      

      

        This will permanently destroy the pool and all datasets within it.
        The underlying disks will not be wiped, but all ZFS data will be lost.
      

      

        This action cannot be undone.
      

      

        
        
      

    

    

      
      
    

  







  

    

      
⚠ Force Import Pool

      
    

    

      

      

        ZFS reports this pool was previously in use by another system or was not cleanly exported.
      

      

      

        Only force-import if you are certain this pool is no longer in use elsewhere. Forcing import on an active pool can cause data corruption.
      

      

        
        
      

    

    

      
      
    

  







  

    

      
⬇ Import ZFS Pool

      
    

    

      

      

    

    

      
      
    

  







  

    

      
⚠ Delete Snapshot

      
    

    

      

      
Delete snapshot ?

      
This permanently removes the snapshot. This cannot be undone.

    

    

      
      
    

  







  

    

      
⚠ Delete All Snapshots

      
    

    

      

      

        You are about to permanently delete all snapshots for:
      

      

      

        This action is irreversible. All recovery points for this dataset will be lost.
      

      

        Any data that exists only in these snapshots cannot be recovered after deletion.
      

      

        
        
      

    

    

      
      
    

  








  

    

      
Rename

      
    

    

      

      
      
      

    

    

      
      
    

  






  

    

      
⚠ Delete Dataset

      
    

    

      

      
Delete dataset ?

      
This permanently destroys the dataset and all its data. This cannot be undone.

    

    

      
      
    

  







  

    

      
⚠ Dataset Has Children

      
    

    

      

      

         has child datasets and cannot be deleted normally.
      

      

        Recursive delete will permanently destroy this dataset and all of its children, including their data. This cannot be undone.
      

      

        
        
      

    

    

      
      
    

  







  

    

      
Instant Update Settings

      
    

    


      
      

        
i

        

          ZNAS follows a Continuous Delivery model — like Chrome, features that pass testing reach a release quickly rather than waiting for large quarterly drops.
          Enabling Auto-Update keeps your portal current without manual intervention.
        

      


      
      

        
Version Check


        
        

          

            
Check for new version

            

          

          

            
            

          

        


        
        


        
        

          

            
Update Train

            
Pre-release support coming in a future version.

          

          
        


      


      
      

        
Auto-Update


        
        

          

            
Enable Auto-Update

            
Automatically install new versions at the time and frequency set above. Requires ZNAS to run as a systemd service. Not available with Manual Only.

          

          

            

          

        


        
        

          

            

              
Update time

              
When a check is due (per the interval above), ZNAS will run it at this hour and apply the update automatically if a newer version is found.

            

            

              
              

            

          

        


      


    

    

      
      
    

  







  

    

      

        
🔴

        

          
Signature Verification Failed

          
Instant Update is blocked for your protection

        

      

      
    

    


      

        
What does this mean?

        

          Before applying an update,
          the portal verifies that the downloaded binary's signature matches the embedded public key.
          If verification fails, the Instant Update Now button is hidden to prevent installing
          a tampered, corrupted, or unofficial binary.
        

      


      

        
Possible reasons

        

          

            →
            The release was just published and the signature file hasn't fully propagated through GitHub's CDN — wait a few minutes and click ↻ Check again.
          

          

            →
            You are running an unofficial, development, or self-compiled build that was not signed with the release key.
          

          

            →
            A network proxy, firewall, or CDN cache is returning a stale or modified response.
          

        

      


      

        
How to update manually

        
    
          
  1. Go to github.com/macgaver/zfsnas-chezmoi/releases and download the latest zfsnas binary.
    2. 
          
  2. Transfer it to your NAS:
    
            scp zfsnas user@nas-ip:~/zfsnas
          
    3. 
          
  3. Stop the service, replace the binary and restart:
    
            sudo service zfsnas stop && mv ~/zfsnas /path/to/zfsnas && sudo service zfsnas start
          
    4. 
        

      


    

    

      
      View Releases ↗
    

  







  

    

      

        

        

      

      
    

    

      

        
        
        
      

      

        

      

      

        

          

            Used
            
          

          

          

        

        

          
Available

          

        

        

          

            Referenced
            
          

          

        

        

          

            Snapshot Space
            
          

          

        

        

          
Quota

          

        

      

      

        
Compression

        
Compress ratio

        
Record size

        
Reservation

        
Sync mode

        
Deduplication

        
Case sensitivity

        
Encryption

        
Mountpoint

        

          Comment
          
        

      

    

  









  

    

      
UPS Status

      

        —
        

          
          
          
          

          
        

      

    

    
  

  

    
Loading…

  

  








  

    

      

        🛡️
        

          
ZNAS Security Overview

          
Security features built into the portal

        

      

      
    

    


      
      

        

          🪪
          Session Authentication & Role-Based Access
        

        

          Every action requires a login session. Four roles control what each user can do:
          

            
Admin — full access to all features including settings and user management.

            
Standard — configurable per-feature permissions (11 individual flags).

            
Read-Only — can view all pages but cannot make any changes.

            
SMB-Only — no portal access; account used only for SMB file share authentication.

          

        

      


      
      

        

          📲
          Two-Factor Authentication (TOTP)
        

        
Per-user optional 2FA using RFC 6238 time-based one-time passwords. Works with any standard authenticator app (Google Authenticator, Authy, etc.). When enabled, login requires both a password and a 6-digit rotating code.

      


      
      

        

          🔒
          ZFS Native Encryption — Data at Rest
        

        
Pools and datasets can be encrypted with AES-256-GCM (ZFS native). Encryption keys are stored as raw key files in config/keys/ and loaded automatically at startup. Keys can be generated, imported, exported, or deleted from Settings → Key Management.

      


      
      

        

          🧱
          Every admin command using sudo
        

        
ZNAS requires elevated privileges for ZFS, disk, SMB, NFS and system operations. Three modes are available to control how that access is granted:

        

          

            
sudo:all — Recommended for normal deployments

            
A single line grants the ZNAS account unrestricted passwordless sudo. Completely frictionless: no approval steps when new features are installed or when ZNAS is updated. Use this unless you have a specific compliance requirement.

          

          

            
UI Managed Sudoers — Easy incremental approval

            
ZNAS manages its own sudoers file and shows a diff whenever a new feature introduces a new command. Each command is individually approved or silenced from the portal UI. Good when you want visibility without full manual control.

          

          

            
Manual root-only sudoers — High security environments

            
ZNAS has no write access to its own sudoers file. All changes must be made manually by the root user. Requires a manual sudoers edit after every ZNAS update that introduces new commands. Use in environments where every privilege grant must go through a change-control process.

          

        

      


    

    

      
    

  







  

    

      

        🔄
        
Automatic OS Updates

      

      
    

    

      

        Debian and Ubuntu ship an unattended-upgrades service that can install
        security (and sometimes regular) package updates on its own, in the background,
        on a daily systemd timer — without you initiating it from this page.
      


      

        
⚠ Why this matters on a NAS

        

          When an update lands, packages may be reconfigured and services restarted — Samba,
          NFS, iSCSI, the ZFS kernel modules, or even this portal. That can mean a
          brief, unannounced interruption of file shares and running VMs/containers, at a
          time you did not choose. A kernel upgrade can also leave the running kernel out of sync with the
          loaded ZFS module until the next reboot.
        

      


      

        

          ✅
          

            
Recommended for a NAS: Disabled

            

              Keep automatic upgrades off and install updates yourself from this page,
              during a maintenance window when a short service blip is acceptable. You stay in control of
              when services restart.
            

          

        

        

          ⚙️
          

            
How the toggle works

            

              The gear next to the status line writes
              /etc/apt/apt.conf.d/20auto-upgrades
              — the same setting dpkg-reconfigure unattended-upgrades
              manages — flipping the daily background upgrade on or off.
            

          

        

      

    

  







  

    

      

        🔄
        
Automatic OS Updates

      

      
    

    

      


      

        The unattended-upgrades
        package is not installed, so the OS cannot auto-install updates. There is nothing to
        disable — your system only updates when you do it from this page.
      


      

        

          

            
Auto-install updates in the background

            

          

          
        


        

          💡
          

            Recommended for a NAS: keep this OFF.
            Install updates manually from this page during a maintenance window so you control when shares,
            VMs, and containers briefly restart. Learn more
          

        

      

    

  






  

    

      

        🔋
        

          
UPS Management

          BETA
        

      

      
    

    


      

        
⚡ Auto-Detection & Auto-Configuration

        

          Installing this feature automatically scans all USB buses for a connected UPS and configures the NUT daemon on your behalf. No manual configuration needed in most cases.
        

      


      

        

          📦
          

            
What gets installed

            
The nut and nut-client packages (NUT — Network UPS Tools). Two systemd services are started: nut-server (upsd) and nut-client (upsmon).

          

        

        

          🔍
          

            
Auto-detection at install time

            
After install, nut-scanner probes all USB buses. If a UPS is found, NUT is fully configured automatically and the battery widget appears in the top bar.

          

        

        

          🔄
          

            
Changing your UPS hardware

            
If you replace or swap your UPS device, uninstall and re-install this feature to trigger a fresh auto-detection and configuration. You can also trigger a manual re-scan from the UPS panel in the top bar.

          

        

        

          🛡️
          

            
Shutdown protection

            
Configure battery percentage and/or runtime thresholds from the UPS panel. The portal monitors the UPS directly — a graceful system shutdown is triggered only after 20+ consecutive seconds on battery power and your threshold is crossed.

          

        

      


      

        ⚠ Note: Your UPS must be connected via USB before installing, so the scanner can detect it. Hot-plug detection after install requires a manual re-scan.
      

    

    

      
    

  








  

    

      
Push VM to InterLink Server — 

      
    

    

      
      

        

          

            
            
          

          

            
            
          

          

            
            
            
⚠ This ID is already in use on the remote server

          

          

            
            
            
⚠ This description is already in use on the remote server

          

          

            
            

              

                Local NIC
                Remote Bridge
              

              
            

          

          

            ⚠ LXD trust not configured for this server.
            
          

          

            ⚠ This VM has a virtual TPM device. The destination must have swtpm installed (apt install swtpm) or the VM will fail to start.
          

          

            ⚠ Instance must be stopped before pushing.
          

        

      

      
      

        
Preparing…

        

          

        

        

        

      

    

    

      
      
    

    

      
      
      
    

  






  

    

      
Shutdown or Kill — 

      
    

    

      
A graceful shutdown signal will be sent. If the VM has not stopped within the timeout, it will be force-killed.

      

        
        
      

    

    

      
      
    

  







  

    

      
VMs & Containers Support

      
    


    
    

      

        This feature installs LXD — the system container and virtual machine manager — and performs the initial configuration required for VMs & Containers mode in ZNAS. It will install LXD and the QEMU virtualisation stack from the system repositories; initialise the LXD daemon with a ZFS storage backend on the selected pool; configure a dedicated NAT bridge (host-nat) for container and VM internet access; create a physical NIC bridge (vmbr0, vmbr1, …) for each active network interface; and install chrony for accurate time synchronisation inside VMs.
        


        ⚠ The network bridge step rewrites /etc/network/interfaces and restarts the networking service. Your SSH session may briefly disconnect.
      


      
Prerequisites

      

        

          ⋯
          Full sudo access (sudo all) — required to enable 
          
          
        

        

          ⋯
          Sudoers configuration allows feature setup 
          
        

        

          ⋯
          Primary interface uses a static IP 
          
          
        

        

          ⋯
          Network uses /etc/network/interfaces 
          
          
        

        

          ⋯
          Operating System 
          
        

        

          ⋯
          Hardware Requirements 
          
        

        

          ⋯
          At least one ZFS storage pool exists 
          
        

      


      

        
        
        Re-check
      


      


      

        
        
      

    


    
    

      
Steps

      

      
Output

      

      
      

        

          
          Setting up… please wait. Do not close this window.
        

      

    


    
    

      

      

      
    

  







  

    

      
NTP Servers

      
    

    

      

      

        One entry per line. Use server <host> iburst
        or pool <host> iburst.
      

      

        
        
      

    

    

      
      
    

  







  

    

      
Upload Certificate

      
    

    

      

      

        
        
      

      

        
        
      

      

        
        
      

    

    

      
      
    

  








  

    

      
⚙ Pool Settings

      
    

    

      

      
These settings apply to the pool's root dataset and are inherited by all datasets unless overridden.


      

        

          
          

        

        

          
          

        

        

          
          

        

        

          
          

        

      

    

    
    

      

        

          🔒
          Encryption Key Not Loaded
        

        

          
Select a key to load and mount this pool.

          

            
            

              
              
            

          

        

      

    

    

      
      
    

  






  

    

      
⚡ L2ARC Cache Config

      
    

    

      

        ℹ️
        

          L2ARC (Level 2 Adaptive Replacement Cache) is a read cache stored on a fast device (SSD or NVMe). ZFS automatically promotes hot data to L2ARC to reduce latency for repeated reads.


          Best disk type: NVMe or high-endurance SSD. Capacity ideally ≥ your working data set (not RAM). Low-endurance SSDs will wear out quickly under heavy random read workloads.


          Warning: Removing a cache device immediately evicts all its cached data — this does not cause data loss but may temporarily increase read latency.
        

      

      

      
Current Cache Devices

      

      
Add Cache Device

      

        
        
      

    

    

      
    

  







  

    

      
🧠 ARC Level 1 Cache (Memory)

      
    

    

      
      

        ⚠
        

          Advanced Setting — Modify with care.

          ARC is ZFS's adaptive read cache in RAM. Incorrect tuning can reduce performance or cause out-of-memory conditions.
          Changes take effect immediately and persist across reboots via /etc/modprobe.d/zfs.conf.
          Only adjust these values if you have a specific reason to override the kernel defaults.
        

      


      


      
      
Current State

      

        

        

        

        

      


      
      
Tunable Parameters

      

        

          
          

            
            

          

          

        

        

          
          
          

        

      

      


      
      
Cache Breakdown
        
      

      


      
      
Statistics

      

    

    

      
      
    

  







  

    

      
⬆ Upgrade ZFS Pool

      
    

    

      

      

        This will upgrade your pool to support all available ZFS feature flags on this system.
      

      

        After the upgrade, pools with newer features may not be importable by older ZFS versions. Ensure any other systems that access this pool are also running a compatible ZFS version.
      

      

        This operation is irreversible.
      

    

    

      
      
    

  







  

    

      
SMART Details

      
    

    

      

    

    

      
    

  







  

    

      
⏻ Confirm

      
    

    

      

      

    

    

      
      
    

  







  

    

      
↺

      
Rebooting…

      

        The server is restarting. This page will automatically reconnect once it's back online.
      

      

        
        Waiting for server…
      

    

  







  

    

      
⏻ Shutdown Initiated

      
    

    

      
⏻

      

        The server is shutting down. The portal will become unavailable shortly.


        You will need physical access or a remote power switch to turn it back on.
      

    

    

      
    

  







  

    

      
Remove Cache Device

      
    

    

      

        Remove  from the L2ARC cache?
      

      

        All cached data on this device will be evicted immediately. No data loss will occur, but read latency may temporarily increase until the cache warms up on another device.
      

    

    

      
      
    

  







  

    

      
Add User

      
    

    

    

      

      

      

        
        
      

      
      

        
        

          
          
        

        
        
Capabilities

        

          
          
          
          
          
          
          
          
          
          
          
        

        

          

            
            
            
            
            
            
            
            
            
          

          

            
            
            
Only VMs/containers whose ID matches this regular expression are shown to this user. Leave blank to allow all.

          

        

      

      

        
        
      

      
      
      

        
        

          

            

              
              
            

            

              
              
            

          

        

      

      

        
        
      

    

  







  

    

      
About API Keys

      
    

    

      
API keys grant read-only access to this portal's REST API (/api/v2.0/…). They can be used by any tool that supports bearer token authentication.

      
Compatible integrations

      
This API is compatible with the TrueNAS widget on homepage.dev. Add the following to your services.yaml:

      
- My NAS:
    href: https://<your-nas-ip>:8443
    widget:
      type: truenas
      url: https://<your-nas-ip>:8443
      key: <your-api-key>
      version: 1
      enablePools: true

      
version: 1 forces HTTP REST mode (WebSocket is not supported).

      
Available endpoints

      

        GET /api/v2.0/alert/list

        GET /api/v2.0/system/info

        GET /api/v2.0/system/version

        GET /api/v2.0/pool

        GET /api/v2.0/pool/dataset

        GET /api/v2.0/pool/snapshottask

        GET /api/v2.0/snapshot

        GET /api/v2.0/disk

        GET /api/v2.0/sharing/smb

        GET /api/v2.0/sharing/nfs

        GET /api/v2.0/service
      

    

    

      
    

  







  

    

      
Generate API Key

      
    

    
    

      

      

        
        
      

      

        
        
      

    

    
    

      

        Copy this key now — it will not be shown again.
      

      
      

        
        
      

      

        Use this key in your homepage services.yaml as key: with version: 1.
      

      

        
      

    

  







  

    

      
⚠ Delete API Key

      
    

    

      

      

        You are about to permanently delete the API key .
      

      

        Any integration using this key — such as a homepage widget — will immediately stop working.
      

      
This action cannot be undone. You can generate a new key at any time.

Role cannot be changed for your own account.

Only VMs/containers whose ID matches this regular expression are shown to this user. Leave blank to allow all.

          

        

      

      

        
        
      

      

        SMB-Only users do not have a portal password. Use "Set SMB Password" to manage their Samba credentials.

Or enter manually:

            
          

          

            
            
          

          

          
Code verified — click Save Changes to enable 2FA.

        

      

    

    
▼

    

    

      
      
    

  







  

    

      
SMB Global Settings

      
    

    

      

      

      

        
        
        
Must match the workgroup on your Windows clients. Default: WORKGROUP.

      

      

        
        
        
Applied immediately to smb.conf and reloads Samba. Default: 100.

      

      

        
        
        
User home directories will be created inside this dataset when the user has 'Home User Folder' enabled.

      

      

        
        
Optimises TCP socket buffers for throughput. Requires a Samba restart for existing connections.

      

      

        
        
Removes [printers], [print$], and any distro-default [homes] outside the ZFS NAS managed block. This action is permanent and cannot be undone.

      

      
      

        

          ▶
          Advanced Settings
        

        

          

            
            
            
Added to our [global] section. One parameter per line. Section headers are ignored.

          

        

      

    

    

      
      
    

  







  

    

      

        
Set SMB Password

        

      

      
    

    

    

    

      Creates a Linux system account (if needed) and registers the user with Samba so they can access SMB shares.
    

    

      

        
        
      

      

        
        
      

    

  







  

    

      
Delete User

      
    

    

      

      

        You are about to permanently delete the user .
      

      
This will remove their portal account. SMB credentials and home folder data are not automatically deleted.

    

    

      
      
    

  







  

    

      
Username already exists on the system

      
    

    

      

        A Linux account named  already exists on this system with:
      

      

        
UID: 

        
GID: 

      

      

        Continue to create the portal user reusing this existing UID/GID. The Linux account will not be re-created; only the portal user record and Samba password are added. Choose Cancel to pick a different username.
      

    

    

      
      
    

  







  

    

      
Delete failed

      
    

    

      

        ZFS refused to destroy :
      

      

      

        Force delete runs zfs destroy -Rf which:
      

      
    
        
  • Recursively destroys all child datasets, snapshots, and clones.
    • 
        
  • Force-unmounts any active mounts and releases zvol device holds.
    • 
      

      

        This cannot be undone. Any clones depending on this dataset will also be destroyed.
      

    

    

      
      
    

  







  

    
Create Dataset

    

    

      

        
        
      

      

        
        
      

      

        
        

          
          
          
        

      

      

        
        

          
          
        

      

      

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

      

      
      

        

          🔒
          Advanced Security
          ▶
        

        

          

            
            
          

          

            
            

              
              
            

          

          
          

            
⚠ Your Responsibility — Read Before Continuing

            

              ZNAS will not store this key. If you lose it, your data will be permanently inaccessible with no recovery possible. Save this key in a password manager or secure vault before clicking Create.
            

            

              
              
              
            

            
          

        

      

      

        
        
      

      

        
        
      

    

  







  

    

      
🔒 Lock Dataset

      
    

    

      

      

        The dataset will be unmounted and its encryption key will be unloaded from memory.
      

      

        All data will be inaccessible until you unlock it again. Make sure no applications are actively using this dataset.
      

      
      

        

          
          Disconnecting clients and retrying…
        

      

      
      

        

          ⚠
          Dataset is busy — active clients connected
        

        

          

          One or more SMB or NFS clients are currently connected to this dataset. You can force-disconnect all active clients and lock the dataset immediately — this will interrupt any ongoing file transfers.
        

      

    

    

      
      
      
    

  







  

    

      

        
🔓 Unlock Dataset

        

      

      
    

    

      

      
      

        
Select the stored key for this dataset.

        

          
          

            
            
          

        

      

      
      

        
        

          
          
        

        
        

          

            This dataset uses a client key that is not stored on ZNAS. Type or paste the key you saved when this dataset was created. It will be used once and discarded immediately — never written to disk or logs.
          

          

            
            

              
              
            

          

        

        
        

          

            Select a key stored on this server. The dataset will be unlocked and the key will be linked — future unlocks will happen automatically at boot.
          

          

            
            

              
              
            

          

        

      

    

    

      
      
    

  







  

    

      
Edit Dataset

      
    

    

    

      

        
        

          
          
          
        

      

      

        
        

          
          
        

      

      

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

      

      
      

        

          🔒
          Encryption Key Not Loaded
        

        

          
Select a key to load and mount this dataset.

New Snapshot Policy

        
      

      
    

    

      

        

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

        

          

            
            
          

          

            
            
          

        

        

          

            

              
              
            

            

              
              
            

            

              
              
            

            

              
              
            

          

        

        

        
        

          
          

            
            

              

                
                
              

              
              

                

                  

                    
                    
                  

                  

                    
                    
                  

                

              

              
              

                

                  
                  
                

                

                  
                  
                

                

                  
                  
                

              

              

                
                
              

            

          

        


        
        

          
          

            
            

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
                
              

              

                
                
              

            

          

        

      

      

        
        
      

    

  







  

    

      

        i
        
ZFS Replication over SSH — How it works

      

      
    

    

      
ZFS replication sends your snapshots to a remote server using zfs send | ssh zfs receive. The first run does a full transfer; subsequent runs send only the delta (incremental), keeping bandwidth low.


      

        
Step 1 — Generate an SSH key on this server

        
Run this on the ZFS NAS server (as the user shown in Remote User):

        
ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -C "zfsnas-replication" -f ~/.ssh/id_ed25519_zfsnas

      


      

        
Step 2 — Authorize the key on the remote server

        
Copy the public key to the remote server (as the Remote User):

        
ssh-copy-id -i ~/.ssh/id_ed25519_zfsnas.pub REMOTE_USER@REMOTE_HOST

        
Or manually append the public key to ~/.ssh/authorized_keys on the remote host.

      


      

        
Step 3 — Grant ZFS permissions on the remote

        
The remote user needs permission to receive ZFS streams:

        
sudo zfs allow REMOTE_USER receive,create,mount,compression REMOTE_DATASET

        
Or use root as Remote User to avoid permission setup (less secure).

      


      

        
Important notes

        
    
          
  • The remote dataset must already exist before the first replication run.
    • 
          
  • Incremental sends require the previous snapshot to exist on the remote — do not delete it manually.
    • 
          
  • The Compressed option passes -c to zfs send — only works if source uses compressed blocks.
    • 
          
  • The Recursive option replicates all child datasets and their snapshots.
    • 
        

      

    

    

      
    

  







  

    
Create ZVol

        and all its snapshots.
      

      

        ⚠ This action is irreversible. All data stored in this ZVol will be lost.

The underlying ZVol will not be deleted — only the iSCSI share is removed.

New S3 Bucket

      
    

    

      

      

        
        
      

      

        
        
      

      

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

      

      

        
        
      

      

        
        
      

      

        
        

          Loading users…
        

        
No users selected = root admin access only.

      

    

    

      
      
    

  







  

    

      
⚠ Delete S3 Bucket

      
    

    

      

      

        You are about to permanently delete the bucket .
      

      

        All objects stored in this bucket will be deleted forever. There is no way to recover them after this action.
      

      
The operation may take a while if the bucket contains many objects.

    

    

      
      
    

  







  

    

      
⚠ Uninstall Feature

      
    

    

      

      

        You are about to uninstall .
      

      

      
Your configuration will be preserved. You can re-enable this feature at any time from the Prerequisites page.

    

    

      
      
    

  








  

    

      
⚠ Uninstall VMs & Containers

      
    

    

      

      
Checking…

      
This will:

      
    
        
  • Stop and disable the incus daemon
    • 
        
  • Purge incus*, qemu-system-x86, swtpm, ovmf, genisoimage, sshpass, bridge-utils, dnsmasq-base
    • 
        
  • Remove /var/lib/incus and /etc/incus
    • 
      

      
It will not:

      
    
        
  • Touch chrony or your time-sync setup
    • 
        
  • Modify /etc/network/interfaces or remove your bridges (vmbr*, host-nat)
    • 
      

      
Re-enabling later will reuse the existing bridges and pick up where it left off.

System error

Delete replication task ?

      
This removes the task configuration only. Existing snapshots and remote datasets are not affected.

          This will register ZFS NAS to start automatically on boot, running as the current user from the current directory.
        

        

          
          
        

        

      

    

    
    

      
      
    

  







  

    

      

        
Sudoers Review — /etc/sudoers.d/zfsnas

        

      

      
    

    

    

      

        
          

            
              

                
                
              

            		
          

          

            Command
            Pending
            Refused
            Approved
          

        
        
      

      

        No sudoers commands found to review.
      

    

    

      
      
      
    

  







  

    

      

        
Enable Auto Sudoers Hardening

        
One-time manual step to grant write access to the sudoers file

      

      
    

    

      

        To let ZFS NAS manage its own sudoers file automatically, add the following alias to
        /etc/sudoers.d/zfsnas
        and include it in the grant line.
      


      
      

        
1 — Add this alias

        

          
Cmnd_Alias ZFSNAS_SECURITY = \
    /usr/bin/tee /etc/sudoers.d/zfsnas

          
        

      


      
      

        
2 — Append ZFSNAS_SECURITY to your grant line

        

          
zfsnas ALL=(ALL) NOPASSWD: …, ZFSNAS_SECURITY

          
        

        
Replace … with your existing alias list — keep all existing entries and add ZFSNAS_SECURITY at the end.

      


      
      

        
3 — Apply with visudo

        

          sudo visudo -f /etc/sudoers.d/zfsnas
          
        

        
After saving, refresh the Prerequisites page — the Enable Sudoers Hardening button will appear in its place.

      

    

    

      
    

  







  

    

      

        
Disable Sudoers Hardening

        
Choose how to handle the existing sudoers configuration

      

      
    

    

      
      
      
      
      
      
    

    

      
    

  







  

    

      
⚠ Samba Restart Required

      
    

    

      
Changing the Windows ACL setting requires a full Samba restart to take effect.

      
All connected SMB users will be briefly disconnected.

      
Unsaved work in open files on this server may be lost. Make sure users are informed before proceeding.

    

    

      
      
    

  






  

    

      
📸 Samba Restart Needed

      
    

    

      
The share has been saved. Shadow Copy (Previous Versions) requires a full Samba restart to activate the vfs_shadow_copy2 module.

      
Restarting will briefly disconnect all active SMB sessions. You can also apply the change at the next scheduled or manual restart.

    

    

      
      
    

  







  

    

      
Samba Restart Recommended

      
    

    

      
The socket options setting was changed. Samba must be restarted for the new TCP socket parameters to apply to existing connections.

      
New connections will pick up the setting immediately. Choose Restart Now to apply to all connections, or Apply on Next Restart to leave running connections unaffected.

    

    

      
      
    

  






  

    

      
⚠ Samba Restart Required

      
    

    

      
Changing the Workgroup setting requires a full Samba restart to take effect.

      
All connected SMB users will be briefly disconnected.

      
Unsaved work in open files on this server may be lost. Make sure users are informed before proceeding.

    

    

      
      
    

  






  

    

      
⚠ Delete Share

      
    

    

      

      
Remove share ?

      
This removes network access only — no data will be deleted.

      now and forever.
No subscriptions, no licensing, no hidden costs.
